author: flowerjunjie
title: Giant monolithic _specs.py with duplicated TreeSitterLangSpec pattern
summary: All three issues target the same file (_specs.py) and describe it as a monolithic, duplicated, tightly-coupled module. However, the file is a declarative data registry — each of the 28 TreeSitterLangSpec instances contains unique tree-sitter queries, comment node types, and import resolvers specific to each language. This is not code duplication; it is inherently distinct per-language configuration data. The 'tight coupling' claim overstates the import count (21, not 25) and importing needed resolvers is normal wiring. Splitting into 28 files would add indirection with no functional benefit. This is a subjective style preference, not an engineering flaw.
notes: File is 801 lines with 28 specs — numbers verified. But 21 resolvers imported, not 25. The 'duplication' is declarative data (unique queries per language), not duplicated logic. A factory pattern would still need all the same query strings. Generic 'big file bad' observation.
